title: A Centralized Pathology Protocol for Global Cancer Genomics
author: Behnoush Abedi-Ardekani
materials: Re-check that the case ID matches all materials.
Proceed to step 10 of equipment setup.
A. Frozen sectioning for pathology evaluation
Follow steps to embed and freeze
Fix slide in 70% ethanol for 2 min.
Air-dry at room temperature for 5 min.
Acquire images before and after microdissection.

steps.title: Part 1
steps.instruction: Part 1: Embedding of frozen tissues in the Optimal Cutting Temperature (OCT) medium and cryosectioning

steps.title: Remove the cryotubes containing frozen tissues...
steps.instruction: Remove the cryotubes containing frozen tissues from deep freezer (– 80° or – 20° C) according to the list provided by the data manager.

steps.title: In Mutographs, a dedicated data manager...
steps.instruction: In Mutographs, a dedicated data manager co-ordinated the circuit of procedures in

steps.title: regular manner and as a quality control measurement
steps.instruction: regular manner and as a quality control measurement. Institutional and project-based design might act differently.

steps.title: Verify the identification number of each cryotube
steps.instruction: Verify the identification number of each cryotube. CRITICAL STEP: Always confirm sample ID. Apply the unique ID to all equipment (glass slides, cryotubes, OCT blocks, etc.) and by-products using your institutional sample tracking system.

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP: Always carry the cryotubes containing tissues from the biobank to a manipulation area, such as pathology laboratory, in a cryobox and in frozen state, by using dry ice (long distance) or ice cubes (short distance). Be careful to avoid unnecessary heat shock. Transfer the cryotubes from the cryobox into the cryostat chamber, pre-cool to -20 °C chamber with maintained – 20° C temperature. Allow the tubes to equilibrium for 1-2 min.

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP: Process only 1-2 cryotubes at a time. Keep the remaining tubes in the cryobox on dry ice, with the lid firmly closed, to avoid heat chock. Select

steps.title: cryomold of appropriate size, slightly larger...
steps.instruction: cryomold of appropriate size, slightly larger than the specimen, to allow a surrounding OCT border.

steps.title: Label the cryomold with the specimen ID using a cryo-pen
steps.instruction: Label the cryomold with the specimen ID using a cryo-pen.

steps.title: Pour a thin layer of OCT...
steps.instruction: Pour a thin layer of OCT to cover the bottom of the cryomold. Allow it to opacify (not fully solidify) for 10-30 sec, creating a flat, level base for tissue placement.

steps.title: Using pre-cooled forceps, remove the tissue...
steps.instruction: Using pre-cooled forceps, remove the tissue from the cryotube and place it on the OCT base.

steps.title: CRITICAL STEP
steps.instruction: CRITICAL STEP: For RNAlater®-preserved tissues, gently blot with a Kimwipe or other lint-free absorbent tissue until no visible liquid remains. Do not wash with PBS, as this promotes undesirable ice crystal formation. Press the tissue gently to ensure even attachment and check that no air pockets remain.

steps.title: Add OCT to fully cover the tissue, avoiding bubble formation
steps.instruction: Add OCT to fully cover the tissue, avoiding bubble formation.

steps.title: Leave the cryomold in the cryostat...
steps.instruction: Leave the cryomold in the cryostat chamber undisturbed for 15–30 s to allow OCT infiltration and dissipation of air bubbles.

steps.title: Transfer the cryomold to the freezing...
steps.instruction: Transfer the cryomold to the freezing stage and allow the OCT to fully solidify (20–50 sec).

steps.title: Proceed to sectioning PAUSE POINT
steps.instruction: Proceed to sectioning PAUSE POINT: The OCT blocks can be stored at – 80° C if sectioning will not be performed immediately.

steps.title: Apply a small drop of fresh...
steps.instruction: Apply a small drop of fresh OCT onto the flat surface of the chuck and place the OCT block on it (OCT bedding).

steps.title: ii
steps.instruction: ii. Place the chuck with attached OCT in the freezing stage (pre-cooled area). Allow it to freeze for about 5 min.

steps.title: iii
steps.instruction: iii. Slide a pre-cooled disposable blade into the holder. Adjust the angle and secure it with the locking mechanism. CRITICAL STEP: Use a new blade per tissue block. Process one specimen at a time to prevent contamination.

steps.title: iv
steps.instruction: iv. Select tissue section thickness by ‘+’ and ‘-‘ buttons on the left hand side. Adjust the thickness based on the procedure and institutional design. We usually trim at 20-30 µm, cut at 5 µm for histopathology assessment, at 10 µm for flank sectioning, and at 15 µm for LCM.

steps.title: Trim the block until a flat tissue surface is exposed
steps.instruction: Trim the block until a flat tissue surface is exposed. CRITICAL STEP: For small biopsies, slow trimming once tissue contrast appears against OCT to avoid losing tissue.

steps.title: vi
steps.instruction: vi. Mount the section onto a pre-labeled, pre-cooled Superfrost™ Plus slide.

steps.title: Always, and particularly when treating frozen...
steps.instruction: Always, and particularly when treating frozen tissues, use Superfrost™ Plus slides to prevent washed-out sections during staining.

steps.title: vii
steps.instruction: vii. Leave the slide on the slide rack inside the cryostat.

steps.title: viii
steps.instruction: viii. After all slides are prepared, remove the slide rack and allow at room temperature for ≥10 min to air dry before staining.

steps.title: Ensure complete air drying to prevent...
steps.instruction: Ensure complete air drying to prevent folding or cracking during staining.

steps.title: ix
steps.instruction: ix. Fix sections in freshly prepared 4% PFA for 10–15 min.

steps.title: Briefly rinse slides in PBS; drain excess liquid. CAUTION
steps.instruction: Briefly rinse slides in PBS; drain excess liquid. CAUTION: Fixation and staining should be done in a hood (laminar flow recommended) in a containment Level 2 lab.

steps.title: xi. Stain sections sequentially
steps.instruction: xi. Stain sections sequentially: Hematoxylin (5 min), tap water rinse (5 min), Eosin (2–3 min), ethanol series (70%, 95%, 100%, 1–2 changes each), xylene (2 changes, 5 min each).

steps.title: We recommend avoid rinsing after eosin...
steps.instruction: We recommend avoid rinsing after eosin to prevent washing out the stain.

steps.title: xiii
steps.instruction: xiii. Proceed to scanning and whole-slide image (WSI) creation for Part 2.

steps.title: ii
steps.instruction: ii. Change the section thickness by ‘+’ and ‘-‘ buttons on the left hand side to 15 µm. CRITICAL STEP: Avoid thickness >15 µm; laser cutting is inefficient and may burn tissue. CRITICAL STEP: Ensure flat, wrinkle-free section in the center of the membrane; leave 1.5 cm margin for laser. Discard any folded section.

steps.title: iii. Proceed for LCM, if feasible. PAUSE POINT
steps.instruction: iii. Proceed for LCM, if feasible. PAUSE POINT: Unfixed slides can be stored at –80°C until LCM.

steps.title: Open the image viewer. o Note
steps.instruction: Open the image viewer. o Note: SlidePath was the image viewer software linked to the Leica SCN400 scanner in the Mutographs project, replacing Aperio Image Scope v12.4.6.7001 after SCN400 being out of support.

steps.title: Open the pathology data registry program,...
steps.instruction: Open the pathology data registry program, according to the institutional set up

steps.title: o Note
steps.instruction: o Note: We developed a specific pathology data registry for Mutographs. The point is to ensure secure and hramonized pathology data registry, by any approved institutional approach.

steps.title: Tumor type, PVTC, and surface size...
steps.instruction: Tumor type, PVTC, and surface size are key components to guide the workflow. Guiding criteria are designed based on the aims and objectives of each project.

steps.title: Report and instruct based on options...
steps.instruction: Report and instruct based on options A to D (Figure 2).

steps.title: If non-malignant tissue, extensive necrosis, ou...
steps.instruction: If non-malignant tissue, extensive necrosis, ou non-eligible cancer type instruct option A

steps.title: ii
steps.instruction: ii. If eligible cancer type and PVTC ≥50%, instruct option B

steps.title: iii
steps.instruction: iii. If eligible cancer type, PVTC <50%, enrichment of tumor content is feasible, and surface size >1 mm2, instruct option C

steps.title: iv
steps.instruction: iv. If pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma (PDAC) with PVTC <50%, instruct option D

steps.title: If eligible cancer type, PVTC ≥50%,...
steps.instruction: If eligible cancer type, PVTC ≥50%, but surface is ≤ 1mm2, instruct option E

steps.title: Annotate the area of interest if...
steps.instruction: Annotate the area of interest if instruct for macrodissecton or LCM.

steps.title: Follow steps 9 to 11 to set up the cryostat
steps.instruction: Follow steps 9 to 11 to set up the cryostat.

steps.title: Verify the case list prepared by the data manager
steps.instruction: Verify the case list prepared by the data manager.

steps.title: Extract the OCT block from -80° C storage
steps.instruction: Extract the OCT block from -80° C storage. Check the case ID.

steps.title: Transfer OCT blocks to the pre-cooled cryostat chamber
steps.instruction: Transfer OCT blocks to the pre-cooled cryostat chamber.

steps.title: Equilibrate the OCT block in the...
steps.instruction: Equilibrate the OCT block in the freezing stage for 10–15 min.

steps.title: Examine corresponding glass slides and WSI;...
steps.instruction: Examine corresponding glass slides and WSI; orient pathologist’s annotations on the OCT block. Keep images and slides visible.

steps.title: Apply a new disposable scalpel and...
steps.instruction: Apply a new disposable scalpel and gradually cut off unwanted areas using shallow cuts first. CAUTION: Place the OCT block on a solid, even surface; cutting a mispositioned block risks hand injury. CAUTION: Never hold the block with your hand. Use forceps and keep hands away from the cutting area.

steps.title: Deepen cuts after delineating the area with shallow lines
steps.instruction: Deepen cuts after delineating the area with shallow lines.

steps.title: Allow the dissected OCT block to...
steps.instruction: Allow the dissected OCT block to slightly thaw, remove, and place on a clean cryomold.

steps.title: Identify the post-macro OCT block according to institutional tracking
steps.instruction: Identify the post-macro OCT block according to institutional tracking.

steps.title: Proceed to sectioning, post-macro WSI creation,...
steps.instruction: Proceed to sectioning, post-macro WSI creation, and thick sectioning for DNA extraction (steps A i to A xiv and B i to v). PAUSE POINT: Post-macro block can be stored at –80°C if not immediately sectioned. CAUTION: Discard surgical scalpels in sharps container.

steps.title: Follow steps 1-4 for reagent set...
steps.instruction: Follow steps 1-4 for reagent set up and 12-13 for equipment set up

steps.title: Check the slide ID based on...
steps.instruction: Check the slide ID based on the list of cases provided by data manager.

steps.title: Dip the slide for 10 sec...
steps.instruction: Dip the slide for 10 sec in 1% cresyl violet acetate working solution.

steps.title: Load stained slide into the slide holder
steps.instruction: Load stained slide into the slide holder.

steps.title: Load collection tube (adhesive cap, 500 µL)
steps.instruction: Load collection tube (adhesive cap, 500 µL).

steps.title: Follow pathologist guidance to annotate areas...
steps.instruction: Follow pathologist guidance to annotate areas of interest using the software drawing tool.

steps.title: Start laser beam to cut and...
steps.instruction: Start laser beam to cut and catapult material into the cap. CRITICAL STEP: Calibrate laser, align tube, avoid air currents. Check alignment regularly. Scan slide at end to confirm collection. Apply additional laser power if needed.

steps.title: Remove tube, close, seal, label, and...
steps.instruction: Remove tube, close, seal, label, and store at –20°C until DNA extraction.

steps.title: To avoid DNA contamination, PEN slides...
steps.instruction: To avoid DNA contamination, PEN slides must be UV crosslinked for 30 min. In out institute, certified DNase/RNase-free PEN slides were used.

steps.title: Use a new scalpel for each...
steps.instruction: Use a new scalpel for each sample to avoid DNA contamination.
